lastReleaseMessage: Restore dates.ts after a botched regex replacement in the...

barRaise.topFinding: Twelfth daily run, sixth day with HEAD frozen at 890b493 (2026-06-13, six days cold), same single HIGH. Two things changed since 06-18 and both cut the same way. The tree is no longer byte-identical: the 06-19 canonical audit applied its DESIGN.md v0.3.0 date fix into the working copy, so git diff --stat grew 13/6 to 14/7; a second drift-fix landed in the tree without shipping. And the uncommitted docs/DESIGN.md diff is not the cosmetic rebrand the 06-18 report assumed: it retracts a false claim that a daily Cron Trigger fires preference_window_opened, which the code does not implement (no [triggers] block, no scheduled() handler), so the canonical doc on HEAD currently documents a non-existent producer. main is level with origin/main (0 0); nothing external blocks a commit. One release.ps1 run ships the rebrand, the date fix, the cron-trigger correction, and git-guard.ps1, closes the HIGH, and reverts the verdict to healthy.

actions.title: Ship the whole working tree in one pass: `cd X:\YesAndScheduler; .\scripts\release.ps1` (or `git add -A; git commit`). Lands the rebrand, the DESIGN v0.3.0 date fix, the cron-trigger correction, and `git-guard.ps1`. Nothing external blocks it (origin/main...HEAD = 0 0). Closes the reliability HIGH, the maintainability and audit-loop MEDIUMs, and the observability/version-pill cross-references at once.
Pause `bar-raise-scheduler-daily`, or drop it to twice-weekly, until HEAD moves. The recommendation has ridden eight reports; apply it to the task registry instead of re-deriving it a twelfth time.
